skill.name: analytics-tracking
skill.description: Design, audit, and improve analytics tracking systems for GA4, GTM, product analytics, conversion tracking, and UTM governance.
skill.summary: Create reliable measurement plans and validation workflows for analytics tracking.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives caused by analytics terminology and a Markdown event naming example. No executable scripts, shell commands, weak cryptographic usage, reconnaissance behavior, network calls, or prompt injection attempts were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Event Naming Example
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged lines 231-233 as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The content is a Markdown code fence showing the text object_action[_context], not an executable command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are inside a Markdown code block and contain only an event naming pattern. There is no shell syntax, command invocation, or user input execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Analytics Terms Flagged As Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged lines 3, 4, 13, 171, 197, 334, and 367 as weak cryptographic algorithm usage. These lines describe analytics tracking, signals, event design, failure modes, and output tables.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged terms are used in a measurement and analytics context. No cryptographic API, hashing function, encryption routine, or security-sensitive implementation appears in the file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: GTM Guidance Flagged As Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged line 306 as system reconnaissance. The line advises avoiding multiple Google Tag Manager containers and does not request host, network, process, or environment enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is product analytics guidance about tag manager setup. It contains no commands or instructions to inspect the local system.

content.value_statement: Unreliable analytics can lead teams to make decisions from duplicated events, weak conversions, or broken attribution. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design, audit, and validate tracking plans before teams act on the data.
actual_capabilities: Scores analytics readiness with a 0 to 100 Measurement Readiness Index.
Designs event taxonomies with names, properties, triggers, and supported decisions.
Reviews conversion definitions, counting rules, attribution context, and UTM discipline.
Identifies common tracking failures such as duplicate events, missing properties, and PII leakage.
Produces validation steps for GA4, GTM, mobile, browser, and consent-state checks.
Connects tracking recommendations to business decisions and ownership needs.
limitations: Does not execute tracking code or inspect live analytics accounts by itself.
Requires user-provided context about tools, events, conversions, and business goals.
Does not provide legal advice for privacy or consent requirements.
Cannot guarantee data accuracy without implementation testing in the target environment.

use_cases.title: Audit a GA4 Setup
use_cases.description: Review existing events, conversions, UTMs, and validation gaps before trusting reports.

use_cases.title: Design Product Events
use_cases.description: Create a focused event model that maps user actions to product decisions.

use_cases.title: Prepare GTM Implementation
use_cases.description: Define clean dataLayer events, conversion rules, and QA steps for implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Score My Tracking Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess my analytics setup using the Measurement Readiness Index. Ask for any missing details before scoring. Include key gaps and the first three fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Tracking Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a tracking plan for this product flow. Include events, properties, triggers, conversions, and the decision supported by each signal.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Conversion Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these conversions for quality and counting problems. Identify weak conversions, duplicate risks, missing context, and validation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Measurement Governance System
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an analytics governance process for our team. Include naming rules, ownership, change review, documentation, QA, monitoring, and remediation priorities.

output_examples.input: We track page views, CTA clicks, form starts, and signups, but leadership distrusts the numbers.
output_examples.output: The skill would score readiness, identify weak signals, separate intent from completion events, and recommend validation for duplicates and conversion counting.

output_examples.input: We need a tracking plan for onboarding activation.
output_examples.output: The skill would propose events for step completion, feature activation, and onboarding completion, with properties, triggers, and the decision each event supports.

output_examples.input: Our paid campaigns use inconsistent UTMs across teams.
output_examples.output: The skill would define UTM naming rules, governance ownership, validation checks, and attribution risks caused by inconsistent source and campaign values.

best_practices: Start with the business decision before defining events or dashboards.
Validate events across browsers, mobile, consent states, and duplicate firing scenarios.
Document event names, properties, conversion counting rules, owners, and change history.
anti_patterns: Tracking every click without linking it to a decision.
Treating GA4 or product analytics numbers as trusted before validation.
Using free-text properties or event payloads that may collect personal information.

faq.question: Does this skill implement analytics tags?
faq.answer: No. It designs, reviews, and validates measurement plans. A developer or tag manager still implements the changes.

faq.question: Which analytics tools does it support?
faq.answer: It gives guidance for GA4, GTM, product analytics tools, conversions, events, and UTMs.

faq.question: Can it find duplicate events?
faq.answer: It can identify likely duplicate risks and define validation steps. It needs actual test results to confirm duplication.

faq.question: Does it handle privacy requirements?
faq.answer: It includes consent, minimization, retention, and deletion considerations. It does not replace legal review.

faq.question: What inputs produce the best results?
faq.answer: Provide business goals, current tools, event lists, conversion rules, known issues, and compliance constraints.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use this for implementation planning?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill can produce tracking requirements, validation steps, and implementation notes for engineering work.
